meta_title: For Students: testing automation to Solve no version control (Intermediate Case Study)
meta_description: Learn how to testing automation and overcome no version control. A Intermediate-level Case Study using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: Testing Automation to Solve No Version Control
content.seo_description: Learn to use testing automation to overcome no version control. An intermediate-level guide for students using Antigravity and Claude Code for SaaS apps.
content.answer_block: Without version control, your code is a house of cards. The solution is implementing automated testing as a safety net. By integrating Antigravity for test generation and Claude Code for execution, you create a verifiable state for your application, ensuring that every change is validated before you risk breaking your fragile codebase.
content.intro:
You’ve spent weeks coding your first major SaaS project. You’re finally adding that final feature, you hit save, and—catastrophe. The app crashes, and because you skipped version control, you have no way to revert to the stable version that worked ten minutes ago. The screen is blank, your confidence is shattered, and the hours of progress feel like they've evaporated into the void.

But here is the secret senior engineers know: version control is only half the battle. The true armor of a professional developer is a robust automated testing suite. By treating your code as a series of verifiable behaviors rather than just raw files, you can refactor, experiment, and build with the assurance that your system will catch your mistakes before they ever reach the user.

steps.title: Establish a Baseline Test Suite
steps.description: Before changing anything, define what 'working' looks like by writing unit tests for your core logic.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze my current codebase and generate a set of Jest unit tests that cover the primary business logic and edge cases for my core functions.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'generate unit tests for src/index.js using Jest and save to tests/index.test.js'

steps.title: Implement Behavioral Integration Tests
steps.description: Ensure your UI and API communicate correctly to prevent regressions during manual edits.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create an integration test suite that simulates a user login and data submission flow to verify the API endpoints.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'create integration tests for the authentication flow and ensure they pass with the current environment variables'

steps.title: Automate the Execution Pipeline
steps.description: Create a script that triggers all tests automatically before any code modification.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a shell script that executes all tests and halts execution if any test fails, acting as a manual gatekeeper.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'create a script named test-gate.sh that runs all tests and exits with code 1 if any failure occurs'

steps.title: Refactor with Confidence
steps.description: Now that you have tests, you can safely modify your code. If the test fails, revert the specific block.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Suggest a more modular way to refactor my database connection logic while maintaining compatibility with existing tests.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'refactor the database connection to be modular and run the test suite to verify no regressions'

steps.title: Continuous Validation Loop
steps.description: Use Claude Code to monitor your files and rerun tests automatically whenever a file is saved.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Provide a configuration for a file watcher that triggers my test-gate.sh script every time a source file is modified.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'configure a file watcher to execute ./test-gate.sh on every change to the /src directory'

pro_tips: Treat your test suite as documentation; if a feature is hard to test, it is usually a sign that your architecture is too tightly coupled.
Always run your tests in a 'clean room' environment to ensure they don't depend on local state that might break on another machine.
Use TDD (Test Driven Development) for new features; write the test before the code to clarify your requirements before you start typing.
If you have no version control, keep a 'snapshot' folder of your last known good state before running major refactors.
Focus on testing the 'happy path' first, then add edge cases; don't try to achieve 100% coverage immediately, focus on 100% coverage of critical user flows.
content.next_steps: Now that you have a testing safety net, your next milestone is to learn Git basics. Testing prevents logical bugs, but Git prevents file loss. Start by initializing a local repository in your project folder.
content.conversion_block: You have the tools and the methodology to build production-grade software. Don't let the lack of version control stop you—let your tests be your foundation. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.

Ensure your UI and API communicate correctly to prevent regressions during manual edits.
Create a script that triggers all tests automatically before any code modification.
Now that you have tests, you can safely modify your code. If the test fails, revert the specific block.
